description: Free-form labels attached to any entity in any module. Enables flexible classification without schema changes — a purchasing team can tag POs as 'urgent' or 'capex', a CRM team can tag companies as 'vip' or 'at-risk'. Tags are lightweight, user-defined, and module-scoped. Modules never manage tag storage directly.
routine: Tag.add(ctx, input) / Tag.remove(ctx, input) / Tag.findByEntity(ctx, input) / Tag.findByTag(ctx, input)
consumers: any module — tags appear on POs, suppliers, companies, invoices, or any other entity

Tag.constraints: the label value. Lowercase, max 50 chars, no spaces — use hyphens if needed (e.g. 'at-risk', 'capex', 'vip'). Normalized to lowercase on write by the routine

Namespace.constraints: optional grouping for tags within a module, e.g. 'priority', 'category', 'risk'. Allows distinct tag domains on the same entity. Max 30 chars. Null means general-purpose tag

Module.constraints: lowercase module name that created the tag — prevents cross-module tag collisions

CreatedBy.constraints: userId or agentId that added the tag

CreatedAt.constraints: set by MDM routine at write time

postgresql.note: list all tags for an entity
find all entities with a specific tag — e.g. all POs tagged 'urgent'
list all tags in a namespace for an entity type
tag frequency per module — autocomplete and analytics
prevents duplicate tags on the same entity within the same module

dynamodb.note: full tag record — mirrors PostgreSQL. Used for restore and cross-module tag audit

consistencyContract.note: all fields sync — tag must be queryable immediately after creation. No remote-only fields: the full record is small enough to keep in both stores

retention.postgresql: permanent while entity exists — hard delete on Tag.remove()
retention.dynamodb: permanent — tag history preserved even after removal for audit purposes

persistence.note: tags are deleted (hard delete in PostgreSQL) via Tag.remove() — no soft delete. DynamoDB keeps the history. A removed tag can be re-added and will create a new record with a new Id and CreatedAt

usagePatterns.addTag: Tag.add(ctx, { EntityType: 'PurchaseOrder', EntityId: id, Tag: 'urgent', Module: 'purchasing' })
usagePatterns.removeTag: Tag.remove(ctx, { EntityType: 'PurchaseOrder', EntityId: id, Tag: 'urgent', Module: 'purchasing' })
usagePatterns.namespaceExample: Tag.add(ctx, { EntityType: 'MdmCompany', EntityId: id, Tag: 'gold', Namespace: 'supplier-tier', Module: 'purchasing' })
